config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because rights are what protect people from abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, a healthy society is built by respecting personal dignity, freedom, and fairness, not by sacrificing individuals whenever it is convenient. From my perspective, collective goals are important, but they must be limited by clear protections for the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya cenderung melihat hak individu sebagai pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, baik oleh negara maupun oleh mayoritas yang merasa benar sendiri. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi kalau hak pribadi bisa dikorbankan kapan saja atas nama “masyarakat,” itu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan dan martabat orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika kepentingan umum dicapai tanpa menginjak hak dasar individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement because society’s interests should not be allowed to override individual rights by default. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to the “common good” can be used to pressure people into silence or conformity, especially when authorities or majorities claim they know what is best. Collective interests matter, but they must be balanced by clear limits, because once individual rights become easy to sacrifice, abuses can quickly follow. A healthy society is not one that simply puts the group first; it is one that protects people while still working for the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya melihat hak individu sebagai batas penting terhadap kekuasaan negara dan juga tekanan mayoritas, karena “demi masyarakat” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Memang ada keadaan tertentu, seperti keselamatan publik, ketika pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan tidak menghapus hak dasar secara sembarangan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu mengejar kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The statement goes too far, because in my view society’s interests should never become a blank check to override individual rights. As someone from Indonesia, I understand the appeal of collective harmony, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to pressure people into obedience and hide abuse by authorities or the majority. Public safety and social order matter, but any limits on rights must be narrow, justified, and temporary, not treated as the default rule. A society is stronger when it protects people first and then builds the common good on that foundation.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi alasan default untuk mengalahkan hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika Serikat, hak individu adalah batas penting terhadap kekuasaan negara dan tekanan mayoritas, sebab dalih “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Saya memang mengakui ada situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik yang bisa menuntut pembatasan terbatas, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan sementara. Masyarakat yang sehat menurut saya bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan orang, melainkan yang mampu mengejar kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat pribadi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
